meta.ogTitle: Safari Types | Divine Travel Nest Safaris
meta.ogDescription: Every style of East Africa safari, compared — find the one that fits you.
meta.collectionName: Safari Types — Activity & Traveller Styles Compared
meta.collectionDescription: Every style of East Africa safari, compared — by activity and by who you're travelling with.

hero.description: There are two questions worth answering before anything else: what kind of safari experience do you want, and who are you travelling with? Together they shape almost every other decision — park, pace, vehicle and lodge.

cta.description: Tell us about your group, budget and dream wildlife sightings — our team will recommend the activity and traveller style that fits, then build the itinerary around it.

intro.lead: We've been the bridge between travellers and the African wild for over a decade. Here's what sets Divine Travel Nest Safaris apart.
intro.paragraph1: Most safari companies talk as if there's one product — a vehicle, a guide, a checklist of animals. In practice, what actually shapes a trip is two separate decisions stacked on top of each other: the kind of experience you want inside the bush, and the kind of group you're travelling as.
intro.paragraph2: The first — activity type — covers everything from how you physically move through a park (on foot, by 4x4, by boat, in the air, after dark) to what you actually spend the day doing, whether that's chasing the best light for a photograph, slowing down for a spa afternoon, or riding along with a conservation team. The second — traveller type — is about who the itinerary is built around: solo, as a couple, with children, or as a fully private group. Almost every package we run is a combination of one or two from each list, not a single box ticked.

box1.label: 01 — Safari activity types
box1.heading: Safari your way
box1.body: This is about what you spend your time doing — from how you move through the bush (on foot, by 4x4, by boat, in the air, after dark) to more purpose-driven days built around photography, birding, conservation work or simply slowing down. Most trips combine two or three activity types rather than sticking to just one.

box2.label: 02 — Safari types by traveller
box2.heading: Who you're travelling with
box2.body: This is about who the trip is built around — solo, as a couple, with the kids, or as a private group. It shapes pacing, vehicle exclusivity and the lodges we recommend.

activityGroup.description: What you spend your time doing — from how you move through the bush (on foot, by vehicle, on water, in the air, after dark) to more purpose-driven days built around photography, birding, conservation or simply slowing down. Compare them below and combine as many as you like in one trip.

travellerGroup.description: The same parks and lodges, shaped around your group — solo, as a couple, with family, or fully private.

combine.lead: Pick one activity type and one traveller type as a starting point — we'll build the rest of the itinerary, park selection and accommodation around that combination.
items: Gentle morning walks paired with family-paced game drives — ideal for curious older kids.
A dedicated vehicle, no rush, and a guide who understands light, angles and timing.
Join a capped group ride-along with a research or ranger team, splitting the conservancy fee.
Fewer parks, longer stays, and a spa afternoon built into the schedule for two.
